---
title: "About Me"
image: "images/IMG_1407.jpg"

---
<style>
.about-wrapper {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap; /* responsive */
  align-items: flex-start;
  gap: 40px;
  padding: 50px 20px;
}

.about-image {
  width: 300px;
  height: 300px;
  object-fit: cover;
  border-radius: 10px;
  border: 4px solid #444;
}

.about-text {
  flex: 1;
  min-width: 300px;
  color: #111;
}

.about-text h1 {
  margin-top: 0;
}
.social-links a {
  margin-right: 15px;
  font-weight: bold;
  color: #111;
  text-decoration: none;
}

.social-links a:hover {
  opacity: 0.7;
}
</style>

<div class="about-wrapper">
  <img class="about-image" src="images/IMG_1407.jpg">
  <div class="about-text">
    <h1>Hi, I’m Cáit!</h1>
    <p>Student • Analytical • Creative</p>

    <p>I first developed an interest in Business Analytics through my long-standing passion for problem solving, mathematics, and working with computers. These interests naturally aligned when I began studying Business at Dublin City University (DCU), where I am specialising in Data Analytics. From early in my degree, I was drawn to the analytical side of business, enjoying the challenge of using data to understand patterns, improve decision-making, and solve real-world problems. 

During first year, my exposure to Business Analytics tools such as Excel, Jamovi, and Tableau further strengthened my interest in the field. The hands-on and practical nature of analytics modules stood out to me, allowing me to move beyond theory and actively work with data. These experiences confirmed my desire to pursue analytics as a career path and motivated me to continue developing my technical and analytical skill set. 

In my third year, I completed an 11-month placement at PwC as a Risk Assurance Internal Auditor. This role provided valuable exposure to a professional services environment and allowed me to apply analytical thinking in a real-world business context. Throughout my placement, I worked with data to assess risk, evaluate controls, and support internal audit engagements, gaining a strong understanding of governance, risk, and assurance processes. The experience strengthened my attention to detail, problem-solving abilities, and professional communication skills. 

Now in my final year at DCU, I continue to build on my analytical foundation through my specialisation in Data Analytics, working with SQL, Python, CSS and HTML. I am particularly motivated by the opportunity to combine business knowledge with data-driven insights and technology. With a strong academic background, practical industry experience, and a genuine passion for analytics, I am eager to pursue a career where I can continue learning and delivering value through data. </p>

<div class="social-links">
      <a href="https://github.com/cait-oreilly" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
      <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/yourname/" target="_blank">LinkedIn</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
